sourcePrompt: A Vietnamese sampan, the flat-bottomed wooden river boat of the Mekong, about five meters long and barely a meter wide. A long narrow open hull of lapped planks, the bow and stern both rising gently from a low waist, two or three plank thwart benches across the middle, a small section of cargo decking, and a single long steering oar trailing from the stern. Weathered gray-brown wood, humble and graceful.
